description_long: This sculpture depicts a life-sized Triceratops dinosaur, rendered in a pale, stone-like material. The figure is highly detailed, with a textured hide, prominent frill, and three facial horns characteristic of the species. The body is robust and squat, with four sturdy legs and a long, tapering tail. The head features a large bony frill and two long brow horns, with a smaller nose horn above the beak-like mouth. The surface appears weathered and matte, suggesting an outdoor installation. The overall form is naturalistic, capturing the prehistoric creature in a walking stance.
description: A realistic, pale Triceratops dinosaur sculpture with detailed skin texture, frill, and horns, appearing as if in motion.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the Triceratops, highlighting the frill, horns, and tail.
description_view_1: Front view emphasizing the broad frill, three facial horns, and the symmetry of the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the body profile, legs, and the curvature of the tail.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail and back, with the frill and head partially visible.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific environment.
